description:
You are an “artifact-manager” who has just received several raw metadata files that describe binary artifacts living in different repositories.  
Your goal is to filter, normalise and archive this information so that a downstream build-orchestrator can ingest it automatically.

Initial set-up (already present when you start):
1. Directory: /home/user/repos/meta  
   Contains exactly three UTF-8 text files:
   • /home/user/repos/meta/core-tools.meta  
   • /home/user/repos/meta/core-libs.meta  
   • /home/user/repos/meta/experimental.meta  

2. Every *.meta file is a plain text file where each non-blank line has **five whitespace-separated fields** in the strict order shown below (no surrounding quotes):  
      artifact_id repo version size_bytes sha256  
   For example:  
      `a100 core-tools 1.0.0 2048 1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111`

3. The exact contents of the *.meta files present at start-up is:
   • core-tools.meta  
     a100 core-tools 1.0.0 2048 1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111  
     a101 core-tools 1.1.0 1000 2222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222  
     a102 core-tools 2.0.0 4096 3333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333  

   • core-libs.meta  
     b200 core-libs 1.0.0 5120 4444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444  
     b201 core-libs 1.2.0 500 5555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555  

   • experimental.meta  
     x300 experimental 0.1.0 2048 6666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666  

Task you must accomplish (≈5–10 commands in total):
A. Create a new directory /home/user/repos/summary if it does not yet exist.  
B. From **all** *.meta files in /home/user/repos/meta produce a CSV file named  
      /home/user/repos/summary/artifacts_summary.csv  
   that meets **every** one of these conventions:
   1. First row is a header exactly:  
        artifact_id,repo,version,size_bytes,sha256
   2. Include only those artifacts where  
        • repo begins with the literal string “core-” **and**  
        • size_bytes (field 4) is strictly greater than 1024.  
   3. Sort the resulting rows by artifact_id in ascending (lexicographic) order.  
   4. Use a single Unix newline (`\n`) after every line, including the final line.  
   5. No extra spaces, tabs or quotation marks anywhere.

   With the metadata provided above, the correct CSV file **must contain exactly** the four rows shown here:

artifact_id,repo,version,size_bytes,sha256
a100,core-tools,1.0.0,2048,1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111
a102,core-tools,2.0.0,4096,3333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333
b200,core-libs,1.0.0,5120,4444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444

C. Inside the same /home/user/repos/summary directory create a file  
      artifacts_summary.csv.sha256  
   whose single line follows the **exact** output format of the GNU coreutils `sha256sum` command:  

          <64-hex-character-digest><two_spaces>artifacts_summary.csv\n  

   Example pattern (digest shown here is **not** the real one):  
          e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  artifacts_summary.csv

   The digest must be the SHA-256 hash computed over the **final** artifacts_summary.csv you created in step B.

D. Make a processing log so that future operators understand what happened.  

1. Create /home/user/repos/logs (if missing).  
2. Append one new log entry for every major action you performed (directory creation, CSV creation, checksum generation).  
   The log file **must** be named /home/user/repos/logs/process.log and each entry must conform to the following rigid format (one per line):

      [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] MESSAGE

   • Timestamp must be in your local container time and 24-hour format.  
   • MESSAGE is free-text but should mention the action, e.g. “CSV file generated”, “checksum written”.  
   • Use a Unix newline after every entry, including the final one.

What the automated checker will look for:
1. The directory /home/user/repos/summary exists.
2. /home/user/repos/summary/artifacts_summary.csv exists and its content is **exactly** the four-row CSV above.
3. /home/user/repos/summary/artifacts_summary.csv.sha256 exists, has two fields separated by *two* spaces, the second field is literally “artifacts_summary.csv”, and the first field is a valid 64-hex-character SHA-256 digest that matches the CSV file.
4. /home/user/repos/logs/process.log exists and contains at least three well-formatted entries (step D).
5. No other files were modified or created outside /home/user/repos.

Hint: Standard text utilities such as grep, awk, sort, cut, and sha256sum are sufficient; feel free to use temporary files if needed, but they must not remain afterwards.
truth:
Initial filesystem that exists before the agent begins:

/home/user/repos/meta/core-tools.meta  
  a100 core-tools 1.0.0 2048 1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111  
  a101 core-tools 1.1.0 1000 2222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222  
  a102 core-tools 2.0.0 4096 3333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333  

/home/user/repos/meta/core-libs.meta  
  b200 core-libs 1.0.0 5120 4444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444  
  b201 core-libs 1.2.0 500 5555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555555  

/home/user/repos/meta/experimental.meta  
  x300 experimental 0.1.0 2048 6666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666  

The *expected* final artefacts (after agent succeeds):

/home/user/repos/summary/artifacts_summary.csv  
  artifact_id,repo,version,size_bytes,sha256\n
  a100,core-tools,1.0.0,2048,1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111\n
  a102,core-tools,2.0.0,4096,3333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333333\n
  b200,core-libs,1.0.0,5120,4444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444444\n

(The final newline after the last line **is required**.)

/home/user/repos/summary/artifacts_summary.csv.sha256  
  Must be exactly:  
    <sha256_of_csv><two_spaces>artifacts_summary.csv\n  
  Where <sha256_of_csv> is the SHA-256 digest for the file content shown above.  
  The checker will independently compute this digest and verify it matches the 64-hex string present in the .sha256 file.

/home/user/repos/logs/process.log  
  Must contain at least three lines, each matching the regex:  
      ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\] .+\n$

No extraneous files/directories should remain in /home/user/repos other than:
  meta/ (unchanged),
  summary/artifacts_summary.csv,
  summary/artifacts_summary.csv.sha256,
  logs/process.log
